summary: Iteration 277 — Backend test for GET /api/accounts/sku-realized-margin (invoice-based volume-weighted realized gross margin per SKU). 14/14 pytest cases passed. Endpoint verified end-to-end: time-window filtering, response shape, per-SKU raw-sum consistency, COGS math against MongoDB (with city aliasing), Net-vs-Gross ordering, FOC bookkeeping fields, coverage_pct computation, retail exclusion, city filter narrowing, plus regression on /api/accounts/sku-pricing-grid and /api/accounts/stats/summary.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L6861 total_scope_accounts counts ALL accounts (including retail/GOP-excluded) whereas ordering_accounts counts only GOP-eligible ones. Denominator/numerator use different populations, so coverage_pct is under-reported when retail accounts exist in scope. Consider using len({a['id'] for a in eligible}) for total_scope_accounts to keep numerator and denominator on the same population as the review spec requires ('Only accounts with include_in_gop_metrics != false are counted'). Current test tenant happens to have 0 retail accounts so it doesn't fail, but this is a latent bug.

server.py L6917: gross fallback uses `float((rate*qty) or net)` — Python `or` on numerics treats 0.0 as falsy, so a legitimate zero rate*qty falls back to `net`, which is fine for FOC but subtle; consider explicit None check.
Endpoint reads full invoice list into memory (up to 50000) then iterates; for large tenants a Mongo aggregation pipeline would scale better.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing seed invoices (Feb/Mar/May 2026) in test_database DB, tenant_id=nyla-air-water.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Endpoint GET /api/accounts/sku-realized-margin lives in server.py ~L6807. Seed invoices for tenant nyla-air-water are in Feb/Mar/May 2026, so this_month (Jan 2026) legitimately returns empty skus[]; use time_filter=all_time for data. Verified COGS math replays exactly (city-alias aware) via DB read-back. Regression on sku-pricing-grid and stats/summary still green. Only latent code-review issue: total_scope_accounts uses all accounts vs ordering_accounts using eligible — flagged in critical_code_review_comments (not currently causing wrong outputs in seed).
rca of the issue: No bugs blocking; math matches DB exactly across all invoiced SKUs including city aliases and multi-account weighting. FOC/Net/Gross toggles are supported via raw sum fields (units_foc, cogs_foc, revenue_net_foc, revenue_gross_foc, units_cogs_foc). Coverage denominator/numerator population mismatch is the only defensible improvement noted.
